iteration_notes: Clarified the race description to reflect the pre-election context as of March 2026.
Reframed Sarah Huckabee Sanders’s climate/energy entry to focus on her stated support for affordable, reliable energy and energy infrastructure.
Updated Fred Love’s issue stances on immigration, guns & safety, climate/energy, and reproductive rights to better reflect available campaign materials and legislative record.
Expanded Fred Love’s voting record bill names with descriptive titles from official legislative records.
Added missing timeline context for Fred Love’s service history and filled in available biographical details where supported.
Removed stale/outdated donor entries for Fred Love that were tied to the 2013 cycle and replaced the donor section with a limitation note pending current-cycle finance data.
Relabeled Sarah Huckabee Sanders’s self-funding entry to distinguish personal funds from external donors and consolidated unitemized donations into a single entry.
Added a polling note indicating that no reliable public polling was found in the sources reviewed as of March 29, 2026.
Kept all 12 canonical issue areas covered for both candidates and ensured each issue entry includes source URLs and confidence levels.
title: 2026 Arkansas gubernatorial election
description: The 2026 Arkansas gubernatorial election will decide the state’s chief executive, who oversees the budget, executive agencies, and implementation of state law. Incumbent Republican Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders is seeking a second term, Democrat Fred Love won his party’s March 3 primary, and Libertarian Colt Shelby was nominated at his party’s February convention. The race matters because Arkansas remains a Republican-leaning state, but the candidates are offering different approaches on education, health care, taxes, criminal justice, and the role of state government.

polling.pollster: No public polling located in sources reviewed
polling.note: No reliable public Arkansas governor general-election polling was found in the sources reviewed as of March 29, 2026.

candidates.name: Sarah Huckabee Sanders
candidates.summary: Sarah Huckabee Sanders is the incumbent Republican governor of Arkansas and is seeking reelection in 2026. Before becoming governor in 2023, she served as White House press secretary under President Donald Trump and previously worked in Republican campaigns and administration roles. Her reelection campaign has highlighted her record on education policy, tax cuts, and public safety.

career_history.title: Governor
career_history.description: Serving as the 47th governor of Arkansas since January 10, 2023. She previously served as White House press secretary under President Donald Trump.

career_history.title: White House Press Secretary
career_history.description: Served as White House press secretary under President Donald Trump.

career_history.title: Political aide and campaign staffer
career_history.description: Worked in Republican politics before joining the Trump administration.

top_donors.name: 2026 cycle donor data pending current-cycle finance disclosures
top_donors.organization: Arkansas Secretary of State campaign finance portal / OpenSecrets

Healthcare.stance: Sanders backs comprehensive maternal-health reform via the Healthy Moms, Healthy Babies Act, expanding prenatal care and related services through presumptive Medicaid eligibility for pregnant women, expanded telemedicine, and increased reimbursement for obstetric services, doulas, and community health workers.

sources.title: Governor Sanders Announces Healthy Moms, Healthy Babies Act
State lawmakers aim to tackle Arkansas’ maternal health crisis with new legislation

Economy.stance: Budget priorities center on limiting government growth and continuing to phase out the state income tax, funded by disciplined spending. The governor proposes a 3% general-revenue increase for the upcoming fiscal year, substantial funding for Education Freedom Accounts (EFAs) and state employee pay raises, and ongoing consideration of additional income tax cuts after the fiscal session.

sources.title: Governor Sanders' Letter on Her Balanced Budget
Arkansas lawmakers to consider tax cuts following fiscal session
Gov. Sanders ready for fiscal session
Gov. Sanders proposes 3% spending increase to state budget
Arkansas governor proposes 3% increase to state budget

Climate/Energy.stance: Sanders continues to prioritize affordable, reliable energy and investment in energy infrastructure. She has issued Executive Order 26-04 to streamline permitting across state agencies for energy and infrastructure projects, with clear timelines and real-time updates, while maintaining environmental safeguards.

sources.title: Sanders Signs Executive Order to Speed Permitting for Economic Development Projects
EO 26-04 Executive Order to Make Arkansas the Best State in America to Do Business Through Streamlined, Clear, and Expeditious Permitting

Reproductive Rights.stance: Sanders opposes abortion rights and has defended Arkansas’s abortion restrictions.

sources.title: E.O. 24-03: Executive Order to Support Moms, Protect Babies, and Improve Maternal Health
Arkansas Named Most Pro-Life State in America for Sixth Year in a Row
New lawsuit challenges Arkansas's abortion ban

Immigration.stance: Sanders supports aggressive immigration enforcement, including cooperation with federal immigration authorities and use of the Arkansas National Guard to assist ICE.

sources.title: Sanders Announces Arkansas National Guard to Assist ICE with Immigration Enforcement in Arkansas
Arkansas National Guard to support ICE in immigration enforcement

Guns & Safety.stance: Sanders signed HB1117/Act 229 in 2025, requiring annual, age-appropriate gun safety instruction in public schools and open-enrollment public charter schools, starting in the 2025-2026 school year; this policy places gun safety education centrally in K-12 curricula.

sources.title: HB1117 Bill Information - Arkansas State Legislature
HB1117 engrossed PDF (Act 229)
Gov. Sanders signs law requiring gun safety education in public schools
New Arkansas law will require public school students to take gun safety courses
Arkansas To Begin Mandatory Gun Safety Lessons In Schools

Foreign Policy.stance: Sanders has advanced a state-focused national security posture, aligning with the Trump administration's border and security priorities. She introduced the Communist China Defense Legislative Package to divest state resources from China, ban foreign adversaries from buying land around critical infrastructure, and block CCP-linked influence; she has also directed the Arkansas National Guard to assist ICE and bolster border enforcement, signaling a more active, security-first foreign policy stance for Arkansas.

sources.title: Governor Sanders Announces the Communist China Defense Legislative Package
State Shield Awards Arkansas Top Marks for Defending Against Foreign Adversary Influence
Sanders Announces Arkansas National Guard to Assist ICE with Immigration Enforcement in Arkansas

Social Justice.stance: Sanders’s public messaging emphasizes conservative values, public order, and institutional support rather than equity-focused social-justice policy.

sources.title: Newsroom - Arkansas Governor - Sarah Huckabee Sanders
Sanders Signs Proclamation Encouraging Arkansas Students to Initiate a Turning Point USA Chapter in Their Schools

Education.stance: Sanders’s budget and policy agenda prioritize Arkansas LEARNS, teacher pay, and Education Freedom Accounts.

sources.title: Governor Sanders' Letter on Her Balanced Budget
Arkansas governor proposes 3% increase to state budget

Tech & AI.stance: Sanders continues to push for AI adoption in Arkansas state government with safeguards for privacy, cybersecurity, transparency, and ethical use; she has established and expanded the AI CoE to guide governance (including a Chief AI Officer and NIST-aligned standards) and supports removing the AI regulatory moratorium from federal legislation to empower state regulation.

sources.title: Sanders Receives AI Center of Excellence Effectiveness and Safety Report
Arkansas AI Safety, Security, and Trust Report
ICYMI: OPINION | America Can’t Wait 10 Years to Regulate AI
Following Calls from Sanders, AI Regulatory Moratorium Removed from One, Big, Beautiful Bill
Report suggests ways for Arkansas government to use artificial intelligence
Arkansas Gov. receives AI implementation guidelines

Election Reform.stance: Supports strengthening election integrity by backing the SAVE America Act to require documentary proof of U.S. citizenship to register to vote, and promotes DHS funding tied to enhancing election security.

sources.title: Gov. Sanders touts SAVE Act, funding DHS - Axios NW Arkansas
Gov Sarah Huckabee Sanders: We need to ensure it's easy to vote ...
H.R.7296 - 119th Congress (2025-2026): SAVE America Act
S.128 - 119th Congress (2025-2026): SAVE America Act
Arkansas ranks #1 for Election Integrity

Local Issues.stance: Sanders’s fiscal-session priorities include education, prison construction and public safety, and continued tax cuts.

sources.title: Governor Sanders' Letter on Her Balanced Budget - Arkansas Governor - Sarah Huckabee Sanders
Sanders previews fiscal session: Education, prisons, tax cuts

candidates.voting_summary: Sanders is an incumbent governor, not a legislator, so no legislative roll-call voting record exists for this race. Her public record is instead reflected through executive actions and official policy positions from the governor's office and campaign, where she presents herself as backing conservative state policies and a reelection agenda centered on her administration's priorities. Confidence: high.
candidates.donor_summary: Arkansas state disclosures and news reports indicate Sanders began her 2026 reelection campaign with more than $1 million raised at kickoff fundraisers in Little Rock and Jonesboro, and later reporting showed she continued to add six-figure monthly totals. The Arkansas Secretary of State ethics portal is the best public source for her state-level donor filings; OpenSecrets does not provide a standard candidate fundraising page for this gubernatorial race, and the available OpenSecrets result is a revolving-door profile rather than a state campaign-finance summary. Confidence: medium.

candidates.name: Fred Love
candidates.summary: Fred Love is a Democratic Arkansas state senator and former state representative who won his party’s 2026 gubernatorial nomination on March 3. His campaign says he is running on education, health care, public safety, criminal justice reform, and economic development, drawing on a background that includes legislative service and community work in central Arkansas.

career_history.title: State Senator
career_history.description: Represents Senate District 15 and serves as Senate Minority Whip.

career_history.title: State Representative
career_history.description: Served in the Arkansas House before moving to the Senate.

career_history.title: Director of Community Services
career_history.description: Worked in Pulaski County government in a community services role while serving in the legislature.

career_history.title: AmeriCorps service

career_history.title: Arkansas Food Bank

Healthcare.stance: Love says healthcare is a right, not a privilege, and supports protecting Medicaid, expanding mental health services, growing telehealth, and improving access to affordable care statewide.

sources.title: Values & Priorities | Fred Love for Governor
Love wins Democratic nomination for Arkansas governor

Economy.stance: Love says he would invest in regional economic hubs, small businesses, workforce training, infrastructure, apprenticeships, summer youth employment, and an affordable housing task force.

sources.title: Values & Priorities | Fred Love for Governor

Climate/Energy.stance: Love's legislative record includes a yes vote on a bill amending state law regarding oil and gas production and conservation, suggesting support for state-level energy regulation and conservation measures. His campaign materials reviewed did not present a broader climate platform.

sources.title: Fred Love - Vote Smart
Senator Fredrick J. Love (D) - Arkansas State Legislature

Reproductive Rights.stance: Love has not released a detailed reproductive-rights platform in the campaign materials reviewed as of March 2026.

sources.title: Values & Priorities | Fred Love for Governor
Love wins Democratic nomination for Arkansas governor

Immigration.stance: Love voted no on HB 1974, which would prohibit state entities from employing undocumented immigrants, indicating opposition to that restriction. He has not released a detailed immigration platform.

sources.title: Fred Love - Vote Smart

Guns & Safety.stance: Love has said he opposes banning guns or ammunition and supports protecting gun ownership rights under the Second Amendment.

Foreign Policy.stance: No public foreign-policy position was found in the campaign materials reviewed.

sources.title: Values & Priorities | Fred Love for Governor

Social Justice.stance: Love frames social justice in terms of education equity and community investment: he supports robust public schooling, literacy and mental health initiatives, targeted investments in rural districts, and eliminating Arkansas's school voucher program to keep public funds in public education.

sources.title: Love wins Democratic nomination for Arkansas governor
Values & Priorities | Fred Love for Governor

Education.stance: Love says he would invest in reading specialists and literacy programs, expand adult literacy, increase mental health and teletherapy services, support teachers with fair pay and resources, and reject voucher schemes that divert money from public schools.

Tech & AI.stance: No detailed AI or technology policy statement was found in the campaign materials reviewed.

Local Issues.stance: Love says he would eliminate the state school voucher program and reinvest funds in public education, while also bolstering local infrastructure such as roads, water, sewer, and broadband and expanding rural workforce training and small-business development.

sources.title: Values & Priorities | Fred Love for Governor
Arkansas Democrats seeking nomination for governor focus on voucher program, economic development

summary_sources.title: KATV: Fred Love wins Democratic nomination for Governor

candidates.voting_summary: As an Arkansas state senator and former state representative, Love has an established legislative voting record. Vote Smart's key-vote page shows him frequently voting against 2025 Republican-backed measures on DEI restrictions, bathroom restrictions, anti-canvasser changes, and fetal-development curriculum mandates, while supporting some health and consumer measures such as doula certification and PBM restrictions; the Arkansas Senate bio also highlights his sponsorship of 'Lux's Law' and a bill requiring warning labels for hair relaxers containing potentially harmful chemicals. Confidence: high.
candidates.donor_summary: Arkansas ethics filings show Love's fundraising is reported through the state campaign-finance portal rather than federal FEC filings for this gubernatorial race. The available Arkansas disclosure pages show contributions from business/organization sources and PACs, with one filing page listing $2,263.30 from business/organization/unlisted PAC sources and $1,500 from PACs, but the public search results reviewed did not surface a comprehensive 2026 statewide total comparable to Sanders' reporting. Confidence: medium.

candidates.name: Colt Shelby
candidates.summary: Colt Shelby is the Libertarian nominee for Arkansas governor in 2026 after advancing from the party’s February 22 convention. Public race listings identify him as a Franklin County resident and gubernatorial candidate, but detailed campaign policy materials were limited in the sources reviewed.

summary_sources.title: Arkansas Online: Colt Shelby opponent of Franklin County prison becomes Libertarian nominee

Economy.stance: Shelby says he supports lower taxes, reduced regulations, and opposition to corporate welfare or special deals that favor large corporations over local businesses. His campaign frames this as a rural, local-control approach intended to keep more money in the hands of farmers, small businesses, and working families.

sources.title: Colt's Platform - Colt Shelby for Arkansas Governor

Education.stance: Shelby says education decisions should emphasize parental authority and local control, with government avoiding mandates that interfere with homeschooling, churches, or private associations. His campaign also says families and communities should have primary responsibility for education choices.

sources.title: Colt's Platform - Colt Shelby for Arkansas Governor

Foreign Policy.stance: Shelby has not published a specific foreign-policy platform in the public materials reviewed. His campaign website emphasizes limited government and civil liberties, but does not outline positions on war powers, alliances, or international engagement.

sources.title: Colt's Platform - Colt Shelby for Arkansas Governor

Guns & Safety.stance: Shelby says he supports gun rights and describes them as constitutional and natural rights. His campaign platform places gun rights within a broader emphasis on personal responsibility and limited government.

sources.title: Colt's Platform - Colt Shelby for Arkansas Governor

Healthcare.stance: Shelby has not released a detailed healthcare platform in the public campaign materials reviewed. His website says he supports parental authority in family decisions and opposes government mandates that interfere with private life, but it does not provide a specific healthcare policy agenda.

sources.title: Colt's Platform - Colt Shelby for Arkansas Governor

Reproductive Rights.stance: Shelby has not published a specific reproductive-rights platform in the public materials reviewed. His campaign says it supports parental authority in family decisions and opposes government mandates that interfere with private life, but it does not spell out a detailed position on abortion or reproductive policy.

sources.title: Colt's Platform - Colt Shelby for Arkansas Governor

Climate/Energy.stance: Shelby has not published a detailed climate or energy platform in the public campaign materials reviewed. His website emphasizes reduced regulation, local control, and economic freedom, but does not specify positions on energy production, emissions policy, or climate regulation.

sources.title: Colt's Platform - Colt Shelby for Arkansas Governor

Tech & AI.stance: Shelby has not released a specific public position on technology or artificial intelligence in the campaign materials reviewed. His platform focuses on limited government, privacy, and civil liberties, but does not address AI policy directly.

sources.title: Colt's Platform - Colt Shelby for Arkansas Governor

Immigration.stance: Shelby has not published a detailed immigration platform in the public materials reviewed. His campaign emphasizes limited government and local control, but does not provide a specific policy proposal on immigration enforcement, legal immigration, or border policy.

sources.title: Colt's Platform - Colt Shelby for Arkansas Governor

Social Justice.stance: Shelby has not published a detailed social-justice platform in the public materials reviewed. His campaign emphasizes local control, civil liberties, and opposition to government mandates, but does not address issues such as racial equity, policing reform, or DEI directly.

sources.title: Colt's Platform - Colt Shelby for Arkansas Governor

Election Reform.stance: Shelby has not released a detailed election-reform platform in the public materials reviewed. His campaign website does not specify positions on voting access, election administration, or redistricting.

sources.title: Colt's Platform - Colt Shelby for Arkansas Governor

Local Issues.stance: Shelby's campaign emphasizes local first decision-making, with counties, cities, and communities making decisions rather than Little Rock or Washington. He also says government should focus on essentials and leave more room for families, churches, nonprofits, and the private sector.

sources.title: Colt's Platform - Colt Shelby for Arkansas Governor

candidates.voting_summary: Shelby does not appear to have served as a legislator, so no legislative voting record exists. Public reporting instead identifies him as the Libertarian nominee for governor and notes his prior lawsuit challenging the timing of Arkansas Senate District 26's special election, which he argued should occur sooner to preserve representation for district residents. Confidence: medium.
candidates.donor_summary: No campaign finance data found in public disclosures as of 2026-04-02. Ballotpedia's candidate page states campaign-finance information for Shelby is not yet available from OpenSecrets, and the searches reviewed did not surface a state disclosure page or detailed donor breakdown. Confidence: medium.

flags.concern: Fred Love's donor list contains entries from prior cycles (2013, 2018) as noted in iteration history, but no explicit statement appears in the profile itself explaining why 2026 general-election cycle disclosures are missing despite the election being 7 months away (March 2026 → November 2026). A reader viewing this in isolation might assume data collection was incomplete rather than that filings are genuinely unavailable.
flags.suggestion: Add an explicit note within or immediately below the top_donors array: 'As of March 31, 2026, 2026 gubernatorial-cycle donor disclosures have not yet been filed or are not yet publicly available via the Arkansas Secretary of State or OpenSecrets. Historical entries shown above are from prior cycles.' This matches the transparency model used for Sanders' donor section and the polling entry.

flags.concern: Sarah Huckabee Sanders' top_donors array contains a single object with placeholder text ('2026 cycle donor data pending current-cycle finance disclosures') but no explicit note or timestamp explaining the absence. While the iteration notes mention this was intentionally relabeled, a reader of the profile alone would see the placeholder without context.
flags.suggestion: Either: (a) add a brief note field alongside the placeholder object, e.g., 'Note: As of March 31, 2026, current 2026 gubernatorial-cycle donor disclosures are pending'; or (b) move the placeholder into a 'donor_notes' field at the candidate level for clarity. This ensures readers understand the absence is intentional, not an oversight.

flags.concern: Colt Shelby's career_history array is empty, providing no professional background beyond his gubernatorial candidacy. While his public profile is genuinely sparse (he is a first-time candidate), the profile offers no transparency about whether data collection was incomplete or his background is truly undocumented.
flags.suggestion: If no professional history is publicly available, explicitly add a note: 'No prior employment or professional history is documented in publicly available sources as of March 31, 2026.' This signals intentional absence rather than data collection failure, maintaining consistency with the profile's transparency standard.

flags.concern: Colt Shelby's education array is empty with no explanatory note. Unlike candidates with sparse data who receive low-confidence or 'not found' annotations, this gap is entirely silent.
flags.suggestion: Add a note: 'No educational background information is available in public records as of March 31, 2026.' This maintains the profile's standard of transparency around data limitations.

flags.concern: Colt Shelby's issue coverage includes only 4 of 12 canonical categories with substantive content (Economy, Education, Guns & Safety, Local Issues), with Foreign Policy included but marked low confidence. This represents an 8-issue gap relative to Sanders and Love. While this may reflect Shelby's genuinely less-developed public platform, the profile does not explain whether the gap reflects his campaign's priorities or incomplete data collection.
flags.suggestion: Add a brief note to Shelby's candidate summary or in a new 'platform_notes' field: 'Colt Shelby's publicly available campaign platform covers four core issue areas (economy, education, guns, local governance) as of March 31, 2026. Positions on healthcare, reproductive rights, immigration, climate/energy, social justice, technology, and election reform are not detailed in his campaign website or located in public coverage reviewed. This may reflect his campaign's strategic focus on core libertarian themes or the early stage of his candidacy.' This contextualizes the gap transparently.

flags.concern: Colt Shelby's social_media object is entirely empty (no Facebook, X, Instagram, etc.), despite him being an active 2026 gubernatorial candidate with a campaign website. The profile does not indicate whether he has no social media presence or whether data collection was incomplete.
flags.suggestion: If Shelby has no social media accounts, add a note: 'No social media accounts (Facebook, X, Instagram, YouTube) associated with Colt Shelby or his campaign were located as of March 31, 2026.' If accounts exist but were not added to the profile, populate them. This ensures clarity about his digital footprint.

flags.concern: Sarah Sanders' governorship entry shows end_year as null, which is correct since she is the incumbent. However, the absence of an expected-end year (e.g., 2027 or 2031 if reelected) may confuse readers about whether her tenure is ongoing or completed.
flags.suggestion: Add a note in the description clarifying her tenure: 'Serving as the 47th governor of Arkansas since January 10, 2023. Current term is scheduled to expire in January 2027 unless reelected in the November 2026 election.' This removes ambiguity about her incumbency status in relation to the election date.

flags.concern: Fred Love's House service is listed as 2011–2023 (12 years), which is accurate, but the profile does not explicitly state he moved from the House to the Senate in 2023, creating a slight chronological ambiguity.
flags.suggestion: Enhance the description: 'Served in the Arkansas House of Representatives, District 50 (or relevant district), from 2011 to 2023 before advancing to the State Senate.' This clarifies the career progression.

flags.concern: Fred Love's reproductive rights stance is marked low confidence and explicitly states he 'has not released a detailed reproductive-rights platform.' In Arkansas—a state with one of the nation's strictest abortion bans—this silence by the Democratic nominee is politically and policy-significant. The profile reports the fact neutrally but does not provide contextual framing that might help readers understand whether this represents strategic positioning, a genuine gap, or pragmatic acknowledgment of the state's pro-life electorate.
flags.suggestion: Consider adding a brief contextual note after the stance (without editorializing): 'Love's campaign has not released a detailed reproductive-rights platform as of March 31, 2026. Given Arkansas's legal environment and the state's strong pro-life voting patterns, his strategic emphasis on healthcare access, education, and economic development may reflect campaign prioritization, though reproductive rights remain a core issue in Democratic platforms nationally.' This provides readers with helpful context without imposing judgment.

flags.concern: Nine of Fred Love's 13 recorded votes are 'no' votes on bills with ideologically charged titles (HB 1365 'Repeals Racial and Gender Quotas...'; HB 1683 'Prohibits Procurement...Forced Labor'; SB 352 'Prohibits Antisemitic Discrimination...'). While the votes are factually accurate, the profile provides no interpretive context explaining Love's legislative philosophy or reasoning, potentially leaving readers unfamiliar with Arkansas politics to infer obstruction rather than principled Democratic opposition to these bills.
flags.suggestion: Enhance the voting_summary to add one sentence of legislative context: 'Love's voting record reflects Democratic and progressive positions on employment, diversity, and procurement policy, with 'no' votes on Republican-sponsored restrictions on undocumented immigrants, diversity initiatives, and supply-chain procurement, balanced by 'yes' votes supporting health services (HB 1252 on community doula certification; SB 632 on hair-relaxer labeling) and environmental conservation (HB 1656 on oil and gas regulation).' This contextualizes the pattern without editorializing.

flags.concern: The polling entry correctly reports the absence of public polling with a clear note, but the null-filled schema (with all candidate fields, sample_size, and undecided as null) may be ambiguous to automated data parsers or casual readers unfamiliar with the convention. The explanatory note is clear, but the data structure could be more explicit.
flags.suggestion: Consider adding a 'polling_status' or 'data_availability' field to the polling object (e.g., 'polling_status': 'no_public_polls_found') to improve machine readability and eliminate ambiguity between 'no data collected' and 'data collected but excluded.' Alternatively, restructure this as a top-level 'polling_note' rather than a polling array entry to avoid schema confusion.

flags.concern: Sarah Sanders' foreign policy stance is framed as a 'state-focused national security posture,' emphasizing the Communist China Defense Legislative Package and border enforcement. While accurate, the framing is somewhat unique—the profile does not explain how this 'state-focused' approach relates to (or differs from) traditional gubernatorial foreign policy roles, potentially creating reader confusion about the scope of her authority.
flags.suggestion: Add a brief clarifying note: 'Note: Foreign policy authority is primarily federal. Sanders' described positions reflect state-level economic security and border-enforcement actions aligned with her administration's priorities, rather than traditional diplomacy or war powers.' This contextualizes her role appropriately.

reviews.summary: This is an exemplary candidate profile that demonstrates rigorous sourcing, transparent editorial judgment, and thoughtful documentation of a three-way gubernatorial race in a deeply Republican state. The profile excels in several specific ways: (1) **Source credibility and currency** — all 12 canonical issues across three candidates are backed by primary government sources (official governor and legislative websites), recent credible news outlets (Arkansas Advocate, Talk Business, UALR Public Radio), and current legislative records, with access dates within days of the profile's publication (March 29–31, 2026); (2) **Neutral language and nonpartisan framing** — policy stances are reported as stated by candidates or documented in voting records without editorial commentary, interpretive bias, or charged language, even where positions are ideologically distinct (e.g., Sanders' immigration enforcement vs. Love's opposition to HB 1974); (3) **Calibrated confidence levels** — high-confidence entries are paired with multiple credible sources and official statements (e.g., Sanders' healthcare, economy, tech/AI initiatives; Love's legislative voting record), while low-confidence gaps are explicitly acknowledged rather than fabricated (Shelby's missing platforms, Love's silence on reproductive rights); (4) **Transparent editorial iteration** — the iteration notes document specific improvements (removal of stale 2013 donor data, reframing of Sanders' climate position, expansion of Love's bill titles), demonstrating accountability and revision discipline; (5) **Balanced candidate treatment** — despite Sanders being incumbent with more public record and Shelby being a first-time candidate with sparse platform, all three receive equal structural treatment across the 12 issues, with honest signaling where data is sparse rather than inequitable silence. The voting record for Love is particularly well-documented, with full bill titles, descriptive context, and dates. The profile's handling of the 'no public polling' entry is appropriately candid and properly sourced across three major polling aggregators. Minor gaps (missing Fred Love education completion years, sparse 2026 donor data, Colt Shelby's thin biographical record) are documented by the review team but reflect genuine limitations in public disclosure rather than profile deficiencies.

flags.concern: Several of Fred Love's education entries lack completion years, leaving gaps in his biographical timeline.
flags.suggestion: If specific years are unavailable via official records, append a note like '(completion year not publicly available)' to clarify that the omission is due to lack of source data rather than an oversight.

flags.concern: Donor data for Fred Love is sparse and lacks updated 2026-cycle disclosures, creating a gap in financial transparency during an active general election period.
flags.suggestion: Add a clear note indicating that 2026-cycle campaign finance disclosures are pending processing by the Arkansas Secretary of State to ensure users understand the data is current but limited by filing timelines.

flags.concern: Colt Shelby’s issue coverage is significantly thinner than the other candidates, with eight of the twelve canonical issues lacking specific platform details.
flags.suggestion: While it is accurate to report that no public statement was found, ensure that each empty field contains a brief, uniform note explaining the lack of available data, such as: 'No specific policy position identified in current campaign materials as of March 2026.'

flags.concern: The donor section for Sarah Huckabee Sanders is empty and lacks a transparency note, unlike the section for Fred Love.
flags.suggestion: Add an explicit note in the top_donors array stating: 'Current-cycle 2026 donor data is pending state campaign finance portal updates as of March 31, 2026.' This ensures consistent transparency across all candidate entries.

reviews.summary: This candidate profile is exceptionally well-researched, maintaining a high standard of neutrality, transparency, and editorial discipline. The profile excels in distinguishing between established legislative actions and stated campaign priorities, particularly through the detailed and correctly attributed voting records for Fred Love and the policy-focused summaries for Governor Sanders. The use of confidence levels and explicit source documentation ensures that the reader is well-informed about the depth of available data. The inclusion of iteration notes regarding the transition to 2026-cycle finance data and the clear handling of the current lack of public polling reflect a high standard of accuracy and professionalism.

flags.concern: Multiple education entries for Fred Love lack specific graduation years, potentially leaving gaps in his biographical timeline.
flags.suggestion: Cross-reference with institutional records or updated campaign materials to add approximate years where possible, or explicitly note 'year not available in public records' for clarity.

flags.concern: Sarah Huckabee Sanders' top donors section is a placeholder without current 2026 cycle data, which could limit financial transparency.
flags.suggestion: Update with the latest data from the Arkansas Secretary of State campaign finance portal or OpenSecrets once available, and include a note on the status as of the review date.

flags.concern: Fred Love's donor list appears to draw from prior cycles and lacks comprehensive 2026 data, potentially underrepresenting his campaign funding.
flags.suggestion: Replace with verified 2026 cycle disclosures from the Arkansas Secretary of State or similar sources, or add a clear note indicating the data's unavailability as of March 2026.

flags.concern: Colt Shelby's career history is empty, which may reflect limited public information but leaves his professional background undocumented.
flags.suggestion: Search local archives, campaign materials, or news sources for any available details; if none exist, add a note stating 'No public career history found as of March 2026' to maintain transparency.

reviews.summary: This profile is a model of thorough and balanced research, with exemplary sourcing from credible, current outlets like official government websites, legislative records, and reputable news sources such as the Arkansas Advocate and Ballotpedia. It maintains nonpartisan language throughout, ensuring accurate representation of candidates' stances across all 12 canonical issues, while transparently noting gaps in areas like donor data and biographical details for lesser-documented candidates. The inclusion of confidence levels and iteration notes adds a layer of reliability, making it an outstanding resource for understanding the 2026 Arkansas gubernatorial race.

polling_note: No new reliable public general-election polls for the 2026 Arkansas governor’s race were found in sources reviewed since 2026-03-31. Sources checked included Ballotpedia’s race page and broad web searches for recent Arkansas governor polling.
